---
title: "Alex Knorre"
lightbox: false
page-layout: article

---

```{=html}
<div>
<img src="files/knorre_headshot.jpg" style="float:left;margin:0px 20px 0px 0px;    max-height:40%;
    max-width:40%;
    height:auto;
    width:auto;">
</div>
```

I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Criminology at [the University of South Florida](https://www.usf.edu/cbcs/criminology/faculty-staff/a-knorre.aspx). I received my Ph.D. in Criminology (2023) and A.M. in Statistics (2022) from [the University of Pennsylvania](https://crim.sas.upenn.edu), and then worked as a Postdoctoral Research Fellow at [Boston College](https://www.bc.edu/content/bc-web/schools/morrissey/departments/computer-science.html). Before joining Ph.D. program, I studied Russian criminal justice as a researcher with the Institute for the Rule of Law, European University in St Petersburg. 

My work focuses on victimization, gun violence, illegal drugs, and policy evaluation. I have recently published in PLoS One, Justice Quarterly, Journal of Criminal Justice, Aggression & Violent Behavior, and Criminology & Public Policy.


My CV is available here: [PDF, 120Kb](./files/Knorre_CV_Feb2026.pdf)

My profiles: [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alexeyknorre/), [Github](https://github.com/alexeyknorre), [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=mR-1XBIAAAAJ&hl=en).

## Papers

```{=html}

<script>
document.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
  if (e.target.closest('.paper-entry img')) {
    var overlay = document.createElement('div');
    overlay.className = 'img-overlay';
    var img = document.createElement('img');
    img.src = e.target.src;
    overlay.appendChild(img);
    document.body.appendChild(overlay);
    overlay.addEventListener('click', function() {
      overlay.remove();
    });
  }
});
</script>

<style>
body {
  font-size: 1rem !important;
}

.paper-entry img {
  cursor: zoom-in;
  transition: transform 0.3s ease;
}

.img-overlay {
  position: fixed;
  top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; bottom: 0;
  background: rgba(0,0,0,0.8);
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  z-index: 9999;
  cursor: zoom-out;
}

.img-overlay img {
  max-width: 90vw;
  max-height: 90vh;
  border-radius: 6px;
  box-shadow: 0 0 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);
}

.paper-entry {
  display: flex;
  gap: 1rem;
  margin-bottom: 2.2rem;
  align-items: flex-start;
}
.paper-entry img {
  width: 160px;
  flex-shrink: 0;
  border: 1px solid #ddd;
  border-radius: 4px;
}
.paper-info {
  line-height: 1.55;
}
.paper-info .paper-title {
  font-weight: bold;
}
.paper-info .paper-cite {
  color: #555;
  margin: 0.15rem 0 0.4rem 0;
}
.paper-info .paper-summary {
  margin-bottom: 0.3rem;
}
.paper-info .paper-links a {
  margin-right: 0.5rem;
}
@media (max-width: 600px) {
  .paper-entry { flex-direction: column; }
  .paper-entry img { width: 100%; max-width: 280px; }
}
</style>
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(RefManageR)
library(readxl)

BibOptions(check.entries = FALSE)
bib <- ReadBib("knorre_publications.bib")

# ---- EDIT THIS LIST TO ADD/REMOVE PAPERS ----
# Each key must match a key in papers.bib
# img: path to a figure from the paper
# summary: 2-3 sentence plain language summary
# pdf: (optional) local path to PDF
# replication: (optional) link to replication materials

papers_meta_df <- read_excel("papers/!paper_meta.xlsx")

paper_meta <- setNames(lapply(seq_len(nrow(papers_meta_df)), function(i) {
  list(
    img = papers_meta_df$img[i],
    summary = papers_meta_df$summary[i],
    pdf = papers_meta_df$pdf[i],
    replication = papers_meta_df$replication[i]
  )
}), papers_meta_df$key)
# ---- END OF EDITABLE LIST ----

for (key in names(paper_meta)) {
  entry <- bib[key]
  m <- paper_meta[[key]]

  # Extract fields from bib entry
  authors <- paste(entry$author, collapse = ", ")
  title   <- gsub("[{}]", "", entry$title)
  journal <- entry$journal
  year    <- entry$year
  doi     <- entry$doi

  # Build citation line
  cite <- paste0(authors, " (", year, "). <em>", journal, "</em>.")

  # Build links
  links <- ""
  if (!is.null(m$pdf)) {
    links <- paste0(links, '<a href="', m$pdf, '" target="_blank">PDF</a> ')
  }
  if (!is.null(doi) && nchar(doi) > 0) {
    links <- paste0(links, '<a href="https://doi.org/', doi, '" target="_blank">DOI</a> ')
  }
  if (key == "knorre2024stakeholder") {
    links <- paste0(links, '<a href="http://www.accessecon.com/Pubs/EB/2024/Volume44/EB-24-V44-I1-P6.pdf" target="_blank">DOI</a> ')
  }
  if (!is.na(m$replication)) {
    links <- paste0(links, '<a href="', m$replication, '" target="_blank">Replication package</a> ')
  }

  # Emit HTML — no indentation to prevent Pandoc treating it as code
  cat(sprintf(
'<div class="paper-entry">
<img src="%s" alt="Figure from %s">
<div class="paper-info">
<div class="paper-title">%s</div>
<div class="paper-cite">%s</div>
<div class="paper-summary">%s</div>
<div class="paper-links">%s</div>
</div>
</div>
', m$img, title, title, cite, m$summary, links))
}
```

## Data

I prepared and maintain the following datasets:

Replication package for ``Unequal by the gun: Four Decades of Black-White Firearm Homicide Gap'' (2026) on [Github](https://github.com/alexeyknorre/racial_gap_gun_homicides)

Replication package for ''Measuring Changes in Bias-Motivated Attacks: Evidence from Anti-Asian Violence During the COVID-19 Pandemic'' (2025) on [OSF](https://osf.io/skv69/)

Replication package for ''Comparing risks of firearm-related death and injury among young adult males in US cities to wartime service in Iraq and Afghanistan'' (2022) on [Github](https://github.com/alexeyknorre/safer_at_war)

Russian Crime Victimization Survey, Wave I (2018) on [Dataverse](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/C2OTH9), Wave II (2021) on [Dataverse](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/SGRQTI)

Homicide rates in Russian cities (2020), data and guide in Russian on [Github](https://github.com/irlcode/russian_cities_homicide_data)

Also, check out [the TV Shows app](https://www.tvshowanalyzer.com).
